title: Gratitude Journaling
desc: Write down specific things you are grateful for to engage genuine positive emotion and build a more optimistic outlook.

title: Morning Pages
desc: Write three pages of stream-of-consciousness longhand every morning to empty your mental inbox.

title: Bullet Journaling
desc: A system of rapid logging using bullets and dashes to categorize entries as tasks, notes, or events.

title: Stream of Consciousness
desc: Write exactly what is in your mind in real time, without editing or pausing, to surface hidden thoughts.

title: Expressive Writing
desc: Write about your deepest thoughts and feelings related to a difficult or traumatic experience.

title: Dream Journaling
desc: Record your dreams immediately upon waking to reveal patterns in your subconscious.

title: Shadow Work Journaling
desc: Deliberately explore hidden, repressed, or denied parts of yourself to build self-compassion.

title: Manifestation Journaling
desc: Write about your goals and desired future as though they have already happened.

title: Reflective Journaling
desc: Review recent experiences to draw deeper insights and learn from your own behavior.

title: The Five Whys Technique
desc: Ask yourself "Why?" five times to trace the root cause of an emotion or problem.

title: Art Journaling
desc: Combine writing with visual expression to capture complex emotions words cannot describe.

title: Prompted Journaling
desc: Use specific questions or sentence starters to guide your writing and explore specific life areas.

title: Unsent Letter Writing
desc: Write a totally honest letter you never intend to send to process grief, anger, or resentment.

title: Daily Review Journaling
desc: A brief end-of-day practice reflecting on what happened, what you learned, and your intentions.

title: Nature Journaling
desc: Record detailed observations of the natural world outside to build mindfulness and reduce stress.

title: Travel Journaling
desc: Preserve sensory details, feelings, and memories from a context outside your normal life.

title: Food and Nutrition Journaling
desc: Track eating habits to explore your emotional relationship with food and make mindful changes.

title: Fitness Journaling
desc: Track physical progress, energy levels, and mental states alongside your physical workouts.

title: Financial Journaling
desc: Examine the unconscious beliefs and emotions that drive your spending and saving decisions.

title: Relationship Journaling
desc: Reflect on your own role, reactions, and needs across all of your relationships.

title: Couples Journaling
desc: A shared journaling practice where partners write to each other to foster deeper communication.

title: Parenting Journaling
desc: Capture childhood milestones and process your own growth and challenges as a parent.

title: Pregnancy Journaling
desc: Document the physical changes, hopes, and fears of the journey toward becoming a parent.

title: Legacy Journaling
desc: Write your life story, values, and memories to pass wisdom on to future generations.

title: Recovery Journaling
desc: Track milestones and explore triggers while healing from addiction or compulsive behavior.

title: Inner Child Journaling
desc: Write to or from the younger version of yourself to heal formative childhood wounds.

title: Career Journaling
desc: Reflect on your professional path to process complex decisions and evolve as a leader.

title: Academic Journaling
desc: Process academic material in your own words to deepen learning and retention.

title: Entrepreneur Journaling
desc: Log ideas, decisions, and the emotional reality of building a business from scratch.

title: Project Journaling
desc: Keep a dedicated space for brainstorming and tracking progress on a specific project.

title: Spiritual Journaling
desc: Record prayers, meditations, and inner experiences to explore the spiritual dimension of life.

title: Bible Journaling
desc: Write alongside sacred texts to transform passive reading into active, personal dialogue.

title: Stoic Journaling
desc: Examine judgments and rehearse equanimity to build resilience under pressure.

title: Tarot Journaling
desc: Use tarot imagery and archetypes as creative prompts for self-examination.

title: CBT Journaling
desc: Identify negative automatic thoughts and replace them with balanced, realistic perspectives.

title: DBT Journaling
desc: Track emotional intensity and behavioral targets to build distress tolerance skills.

title: Healing Journaling
desc: Use writing as part of a conscious process to recover from illness, trauma, or disruption.

title: Mindfulness Journaling
desc: Observe your immediate thoughts and sensations without commentary or judgment.

title: Photo Journaling
desc: Select photos and write about their meaning to create a layered visual record of your life.

title: Junk Journaling
desc: Use repurposed materials to create an imperfect, visually textured mixed-media journal.

title: Mind Mapping
desc: Use branching diagrams to creatively brainstorm and make non-linear connections.

title: One Line a Day
desc: Write exactly one sentence daily to build a minimal, sustainable journaling habit.

title: Two-Column Journaling
desc: Examine two sides of a decision or conflict to evaluate them with less emotional reactivity.

title: List Journaling
desc: Write curated lists instead of prose to distill thoughts and generate clarity quickly.

title: Reading Journal
desc: Collect quotes and observations from your reading to build a personal intellectual library.

title: Idea Journal
desc: Capture and develop unpredictable flashes of inspiration before they disappear.

title: Seasonal Journaling
desc: Track changes in your environment across seasons to connect with natural cycles.

title: Medical Journaling
desc: Track symptoms, medications, and treatments to enable better medical decision-making.
